PHM.1.1.text: Paul, a prisoner of Jesus Christ, and Timothy our brother, unto Philemon our dearly beloved, and fellowlabourer,

PHM.1.2.text: and to our beloved Apphia, and Archippus our fellowsoldier, and to the church in thy house:

PHM.1.3.text: grace to you, and peace, from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

PHM.1.4.text: I thank my God, making mention of thee always in my prayers,

PHM.1.5.text: hearing of thy love and faith, which thou hast toward the Lord Jesus, and toward all saints;

PHM.1.6.text: that the communication of thy faith may become effectual by the acknowledging of every good thing which is in you in Christ Jesus.

PHM.1.7.text: For we have great joy and consolation in thy love, because the bowels of the saints are refreshed by thee, brother.

PHM.1.8.text: Wherefore, though I might be much bold in Christ to enjoin thee that which is convenient,

PHM.1.9.text: yet for love's sake I rather beseech thee, being such an one as Paul the aged, and now also a prisoner of Jesus Christ.

PHM.1.10.text: I beseech thee for my son Onesimus, whom I have begotten in my bonds:

PHM.1.11.text: which in time past was to thee unprofitable, but now profitable to thee and to me:

PHM.1.12.text: whom I have sent again: thou therefore receive him, that is, mine own bowels:

PHM.1.13.text: whom I would have retained with me, that in thy stead he might have ministered unto me in the bonds of the gospel:

PHM.1.14.text: but without thy mind would I do nothing; that thy benefit should not be as it were of necessity, but willingly.

PHM.1.15.text: For perhaps he therefore departed for a season, that thou shouldest receive him for ever;

PHM.1.16.text: not now as a servant, but above a servant, a brother beloved, specially to me, but how much more unto thee, both in the flesh, and in the Lord?

PHM.1.17.text: If thou count me therefore a partner, receive him as myself.

PHM.1.18.text: If he hath wronged thee, or oweth thee ought, put that on mine account;

PHM.1.19.text: I Paul have written it with mine own hand, I will repay it: albeit I do not say to thee how thou owest unto me even thine own self besides.

PHM.1.20.text: Yea, brother, let me have joy of thee in the Lord: refresh my bowels in the Lord.

PHM.1.21.text: Having confidence in thy obedience I wrote unto thee, knowing that thou wilt also do more than I say.

PHM.1.22.text: But withal prepare me also a lodging: for I trust that through your prayers I shall be given unto you.

PHM.1.23.text: There salute thee Epaphras, my fellowprisoner in Christ Jesus;

PHM.1.24.text: Marcus, Aristarchus, Demas, Lucas, my fellowlabourers.

PHM.1.25.text: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen. Written from Rome to Philemon, by Onesimus a servant.
